Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Looking at the event logs there are five cases cases 1 to 5 Each case follows a sequence of activities but there are variations For example some cases skip the labeling LB step others have multiple IQC steps before moving on and some re testing RT happens when a product fails the initial test TST leading back to TST again

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1 OR MS QS AS IQC AS TST RT TST PK LB DP D Case 2 OR MS QS AS IQC AS TST PK DP D no LB Case 3 OR MS QS AS IQC IQC AS IQC AS TST RT TST PK LB DP D Case 4 OR MS QS AS TST PK LB DP D no IQC Case 5 OR MS QS AS IQC AS IQC AS TST RT TST RT TST PK LB DP D

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: From these cases I can identify some patterns and possible control flows OR is always the first activity After OR MS is next then QS then AS So OR MS QS AS is a linear sequence

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: After AS there is a loop involving AS and IQC

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Looking at case 3 AS IQC AS IQC AS TST So after each IQC they do AS again That suggests a loop AS followed by IQC then back to AS possibly multiple times

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: According to the loop operator definition if the loop is AS IQC then after AS you can exit the loop proceed to TST or do IQC and then back to AS So in case 1 AS IQC AS TST and in case 4 AS TST both fit

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: RT leads back to TST implying a loop between TST and RT The structure would be a loop where after TST you can exit proceed to PK or do RT and then TST again

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: If the loop is TST RT then after TST you can exit go to PK or do RT and then back to TST That would fit the cases where after TST comes RT then TST again

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: After TST and possibly RT loops the next step is PK But in some cases after PK comes LB which may be skipped For example case 2 skips LB So there is a choice between LB and skipping it

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So after PK there is an XOR between LB and a silent transition to skip Then after that comes DP and D

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: But wait in case 4 after AS comes TST directly So in some cases after AS they go directly to TST skipping IQC This is resolved because the loop AS IQC allows exit immediately after AS

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: The initial AS should be part of the loop first execution This way the structure is OR MS QS Loop1 TST Loop2 PK Choice1 DP D where Loop1 is AS IQC

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: The overall structure is OR MS QS Loop1 TST Loop2 PK Choice1 DP D

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The nodes in the StrictPartialOrder would be OR MS QS Loop1 TST Loop2 PK Choice1 DP D The edges between them are OR MS MS QS QS Loop1 Loop1 TST TST Loop2 Loop2 PK PK Choice1 Choice1 DP DP D

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case4 OR MS QS AS part of Loop1 first step TST exiting Loop1 immediately Loop2 exits immediately since no RT PK Choice1 chooses skip DP D Yes that is correct In case5 after AS Loop1 first step they do IQC loop back to AS do IQC again loop back then exit to TST then RT loop etc This structure should handle all cases correctly
